entries.concern: server-generated idempotency keys would break retry deduplication because retried requests get fresh keys.
plan only specifies version checking at the db write layer; the ui load/state/save path doesn't carry config_version yet.
the shared realtime system doesn't know about timeline_agent_sessions; step 7 hooks need their own channel subscription.
silently reloading agent-originated timeline updates would discard dirty local edits.
create_generation_task is underspecified against the actual task family system.
mid-sentence-cut detection is not implementable without transcript/profile data that the current provider doesn't expose.
removing the duplicate assistant turn may hide message_user text if the frontend only renders assistant-role turns as chat bubbles.
a blanket catch in executetool would silently downgrade db/persistence failures into ordinary tool results, changing the session error/http 500 behavior.

the plan specifies passing pruneselection into usetimelinesave but doesn't detail the hook-layer bridge needed because usetimelinesave is instantiated inside usetimelinedata before usetimelinestate runs.
text clip multi-selections would miss the position tab under the plan's stated rule.
crop and transition controls are not included in the bulk panel, narrowing the original idea's scope.
multi-clip copy is not included despite being mentioned in the original idea.
replacing arrow buttons with drag-only handle removes keyboard-accessible reorder; plan only specifies pointersensor.
no automated tests specified for same-kind boundary rules or save/load persistence of reordered tracks.
the plan's step 3 ontick callback only shows coordinator.update() but the full clip-drag state path (snap, lateststart, ghost positions) also needs re-running during stationary-pointer scroll.
bulkclippanel still imports customeffecteditor; removing it without updating bulk mode would break the build.
removing customeffecteditor makes effects-table entries read-only — users can no longer edit legacy custom effects.
agent chat edits arrive via query invalidation/realtime polling and will bypass the undo stack because those paths are marked skiphistory.
clips with stale or partial params from a previous schema version will not be automatically normalized/backfilled when the effect schema changes.

patchregistry() lacks automated regression tests — the duplicate-id bug's most likely reproduction path is covered only by pure-function tests on the assembler it delegates to, plus manual smoke testing.
if all 3 conflict retries fail, the fallback path risks discarding dirty local edits unless the executor wires it into the existing conflict dialog.
the plan relies on same-track overlap being forbidden but does not explicitly specify the enforcement call (resolveoverlaps or range rejection) in the drop/create path.
dropindicator reads position.trackid without it being declared on the dropindicatorposition interface or forwarded by toindicatorposition().
step 2 discards unstaged edits in 6 files via git checkout head without first confirming the revert was unintentional.
the cold-gpu vlm import crash investigation is deferred — the plan ships the zombie fix without root-causing the specific import failure.
legacy persisted 'enhance' values might exist in storage
no-migration assumption for enhance→upscale is under-supported
validation grep for 'enhance' will false-fail due to video code
grep scope too broad — hits video submode code
enhance/upscale only partially resolved — persisted values not validated
validation scope reaches unrelated video code
no read-side normalization test for legacy 'enhance' values

video-editor-render-pipeline: recurring debt: the revised render-gating plan still uses `cancelrender()` as unmount cleanup and leaves `delayrender()` acquisition described as a mount-time action, which can abort renders or miss initial-frame blocking.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the render-gating cleanup plan against remotion's runtime exports in `node_modules/remotion/dist/cjs/cancel-render.js:46-70`. `cancelrender()` stores an error and throws to abort the render; it is not a benign cleanup for an abandoned `delayrender` handle, so step 2's instruction to call `cancelrender(handle)` on unmount is technically wrong and would abort preview or export with an error.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked how remotion itself acquires delay handles in `node_modules/remotion/dist/cjs/iframe.js:8-18`. remotion's own components create the handle synchronously with `usestate(() => delayrender(...))`; the plan says 'calls delayrender() on mount', which reads like an effect-time action and leaves the initial-frame blocking behavior underspecified compared with the actual library pattern.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the new `useaudioparam()` sketch against `src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileeffect.tsx:1-2`. the plan defines it in terms of `interpolate(audio[binding.source], ...)` but does not explicitly import `interpolate` into the new hook file or simplify it to straight arithmetic, so the helper's implementation contract is still looser than it needs to be.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: checked the supporting ui infrastructure for audio-binding controls. because `src/shared/components/ui/slider.tsx:5-17,31-52` only supports one thumb and `src/shared/components/ui/number-input.tsx:6-33` already exists as the established numeric input control, the plan should call out either `numberinput` usage or a new range-slider component explicitly; right now the supporting ui work is still incomplete in the plan.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: checked the planned test location for prompt-template changes against `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts`. since that suite mocks `buildgenerateeffectmessages()`, touching only `index.test.ts` still misses the supporting test infrastructure needed to validate `templates.ts` itself.

are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: checked the `must` criterion 'edge function tests (ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts) pass with updated templates' against the actual test file. since `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts` mocks `buildgenerateeffectmessages()`, this criterion can pass without validating the template edits themselves, so it is still a weak `must` gate as written.

video-editor-parameter-controls: the revised plan still suggests a dual-value slider for audio-binding min/max even though the shared slider wrapper is scalar-only; it should commit to two `numberinput` controls or a new range-slider implementation.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked the revised audio-binding ui step against the research note and `src/shared/components/ui/slider.tsx:5-17,31-52`. the plan still says 'a dual-value slider or two number inputs' instead of committing to the supported path, even though the shared slider wrapper is scalar-only, so that important review note is only partially addressed.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: checked the verification scope around the edge function. `supabase/functions/ai-generate-effect/index.test.ts:35-46` mocks `buildgenerateeffectmessages()`, so adding only more `index.test.ts` cases still will not exercise the real `templates.ts` prompt content; the revised plan improves scope on the implementation side, but its test scope is still narrower than the actual risk surface.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useclipresize.ts:54-73` against revised step 1. the plan now says to cache `to` in `resizestartref`, but it still never states whether that value must come from committed clip state or from a fresh `getsourcetime(...)` calculation. because the current resize handler computes a transient wrong `to` during left-edge trims before commit-time rematerialization repairs it, leaving `origin.to` implicit is still a correctness-sensitive ambiguity.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked revised step 4.2 against `src/tools/video-editor/components/timelineeditor/timelinecanvas.tsx:483-490` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useclipresize.ts:56-60`. the plan still gives two different formulas for audio `minstart`, and one of them depends on `clipmeta.to`, so the left-edge live-clamp requirement is not fully settled yet.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: checked `src/tools/video-editor/lib/resolve-overlaps.ts:139-195` at the `useclipresize` call site. this api only produces `metapatches[clipid]` for the moved clip, so revised step 3's generic 'for each clip id in overlappatches' loop is broader than the actual resize surface and adds complexity the fix does not currently need.

clip resize math: revised step 1 still leaves the source of `origin.to` under-specified, so the speed formula can be implemented against the wrong `to` value unless the plan explicitly says to use committed clip state or recompute it with `getsourcetime(...)`.
audio clamp math: revised step 4.2 still gives conflicting formulas for left-edge `minstart`, and one of them depends on `clipmeta.to`, so the live audio clamp is not fully specified.
resize hook mutation: revised step 2.4 clamps audio by mutating `action.end`, even though the current resize hook builds new row objects immutably from the callback input.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked revised step 2.4 against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useclipresize.ts:76-100`. the plan still says to clamp audio by writing back to `action.end`, but the current hook treats the callback `action` as input data and then creates fresh row actions from it. mutating that caller-owned object is unnecessary and risks coupling the resize hook to outside references instead of using a local `clampedend` variable.

are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: checked the remaining `must` criteria against the revised verification plan. criteria like 'existing timelinecanvas.test.tsx tests pass' and 'rowstoconfig serialization ... verified by read-only inspection' are reviewable, but they are still proxy gates rather than direct evidence that resize speed math and live audio clamping behave correctly, so the criteria are improved but not fully outcome-oriented yet.

video-editor shell: the plan’s width/aspect-based condensed threshold ignores the existing standard-layout maximize-timeline path, which already fixes the preview row at 180px. in this repo that means portrait timelines could be forced into condensed mode on normal desktop heights, effectively taking away the non-condensed maximize-timeline workflow unless the plan explicitly scopes the new threshold around `istimelinemaximized` or otherwise accepts that behavior change.
video editor lightbox tests: moving `usequery` into `videoeditorprovider` will break the existing provider unit test unless the plan also updates the test harness to supply react query context.
final-video duration handling: `useshotfinalvideos()` does not provide duration and the timeline uses `clip.to - clip.from` as truth, so the proposed in-place trim can overrun shorter source media.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usestalevariants.ts` against the proposed `usefinalvideoavailable` state model: stale reminders are dismissed per `assetkey` and can clear when the asset changes, but the plan dismisses by `shotid`, which would hide any newer final video for the same shot for the rest of the session even though `useshotfinalvideos()` exposes a changing final-video `id`.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the no-duration-fetch decision against `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useshotfinalvideos.ts:19-37`, `src/tools/video-editor/lib/config-utils.ts:17-35`, and `src/tools/video-editor/compositions/visualclip.tsx:114-123`. the repository still derives clip length from `to - from`, and the final-video query still provides no duration metadata, so the revised plan still cannot prove that `to: endtime - starttime` is safe for shorter source media; a short final video can still be asked to play longer than its actual content.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the hand-built replacement clip against the existing insert path in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useassetmanagement.ts:201-252,262-271` and serialization in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usetimelinecommit.ts:210-216`. the revised `timelineeditor` sketch still bypasses current insertion invariants such as manual-track geometry defaults and explicit `cliporder` updates, so replacement clips on manual visual tracks will not be initialized the same way as other inserted video clips and may serialize in a different per-track order.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: checked the existing video-drop paths in `src/tools/video-editor/lib/external-drop-utils.ts:46-55,386-412` and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useassetmanagement.ts:198-252`. the broader repository pattern for video assets is not just “register then insert”; multi-generation video drops also patch `registry.duration` using `getdroppedgenerationduration()`, while `useshotfinalvideos()` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useshotfinalvideos.ts:19-37` currently fetches no duration metadata. that means final-video replacement is broader than badge wiring alone if the new clip is expected to behave like other video assets in timing ui and resize logic.
final-video reminder state: dismissing by `shotid` instead of final-video id still suppresses later final outputs for the same shot during the same session.
timeline media duration: the revised plan still sizes the final-video clip to the shot-group span without any duration source, so shorter final videos can still overrun their real media length.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: checked related asset-insertion code in `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useassetmanagement.ts:150-271` and `src/tools/video-editor/lib/coordinate-utils.ts:24-33`. the broader repository pattern for adding timeline media is centralized there, including track compatibility, manual-track defaults, selection updates, and `cliporder` maintenance, so the revised plan is still broader than a pure menu/ui change and needs either shared helper extraction or a more explicit statement that those invariants are being replicated on purpose.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked the revised dismissal model against `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/usestalevariants.ts:160-166` and `src/tools/travel-between-images/hooks/video/useshotfinalvideos.ts:39-60`. the plan still dismisses by `shotid`, not by final-video generation id, so if a shot gets a newer final output later in the same session the badge would remain suppressed even though `useshotfinalvideos()` would surface a different `id`; that earlier concern is still open.

task reference matching: reusing `taskreferencesgeneration` as the only matcher will miss active video-generation tasks for source clips. the helper does not inspect `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, or `end_image_generation_id`, even though this repo stores and later reads those fields to identify which timeline generations a travel task is using. that means spinners would fail to appear for at least some travel/video tasks.

immediate task feedback: the plan does not cover the repo's existing optimistic-pending pattern, so it does not reliably meet the stated 'appear immediately when a task is created' behavior. with only realtime plus 5s polling, spinner appearance still depends on network/realtime latency.

final-video badge suppression: step 3's proposed `timelinegenerationids` scan over `data.registry.assets` would treat any previously registered final-video asset as 'already on the timeline', even if no current clip is using it.

shot-group badge scope: step 3 suppresses the reminder at shot level, but this repo can render multiple shot groups for the same shot, so switching one group to the final video would hide the badge on other groups of that shot too.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the new plan makes phase 6 (shot drop) and phase 7 (auto-sync) explicitly deferrable follow-ups, but those behaviors were part of the original task summary and idea document as first-class functionality. that is a real scope reduction unless the user explicitly approves shipping the two-tier system without those creation/sync paths in the first pass.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: step 16 resolves the previously-open deletion behavior, but its stated implementation is a post-commit follow-up `applyedit`. in this repository `commitdata()` immediately emits `beforecommit` and schedules a save for each edit, so handling a single delete via a second cleanup edit would create extra history/save churn unless the plan also specifies how that reconcile pass is coalesced or skipped.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: the new `pinnedshotgroupsoverride` design is still underspecified against `materializedata()`. `assembletimelinedata()` computes `stablesignature` from the config it receives, so if the executor literally follows step 2.4 and overwrites `pinnedshotgroups` on an already-materialized `timelinedata` before `commitdata()`, the saved config and the cached `stablesignature` will diverge unless the data is rebuilt from the updated config.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: the revised grouped-drag plan fixes the pointer-down selection regression, but it still leaves `pinnedshotgroup.trackid` stale on cross-track moves. the data model stores `trackid`, and current drag/move helpers only update rows/meta/clip order, so a pinned group dragged to another track would keep pointing at the old track unless the drag commit also patches group metadata.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: cross-track group motion is broader than step 18 currently states. because the pinned-group model now includes `trackid`, every move path that can relocate a grouped clip set across rows has to update pinned-group metadata too; otherwise the overlay and menu logic will still reference the previous track even though the clip actions moved successfully.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: if phases 6 and 7 are deferred, the shipped work becomes a narrower "pin existing inferred groups and toggle them" feature rather than the full brief, which also called out shot-drop creation and shot-image auto-sync. that broader scope tension is still present in the revised plan.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: the move infrastructure is still not fully enumerated for the new `trackid` field on `pinnedshotgroup`. `useclipdrag` can perform cross-track multi-drag and `usetimelinetrackmanagement.movecliptorow()` performs cross-track single-clip moves via rows/meta patches, but the revised plan does not call out updating pinned-group metadata in those move commits.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: the supporting test infrastructure remains under-specified for the new mutation branch. `usetimelinecommit`, `timeline-save-utils.test.ts`, and `usetimelinehistory` all exercise config rebuild behavior today, but the validation section still does not add focused tests for the new `type: 'pinnedshotgroups'` path or for `pinnedshotgroupsoverride` on rows edits.

find the callers of the changed function. what arguments do they actually pass? does the fix handle all of them?: the current `commitdata()` and history pipeline treat each `applyedit()` as its own commit boundary. because step 16 proposes a post-commit reconcile edit after deletions, the actual callers here matter: without `skiphistory` or transaction coalescing, one user action will produce two history events and likely two saves.

find the callers of the changed function. what arguments do they actually pass? does the fix handle all of them?: `handleassetdrop()` and the external-drop wrapper are not pure builders; they immediately mutate editor state and call `applyedit()` per asset. that means the plan cannot literally reuse those callers for atomic shot-drop creation without first extracting a lower-level helper, or it will violate its own "single applyedit" requirement.

media insertion conventions: the plan still says to reuse asset-management helpers for shot drop, but the current helpers are side-effectful and commit immediately, so atomic shot-drop creation still needs a lower-level extracted helper rather than direct reuse.

timeline signatures: applying `pinnedshotgroupsoverride` after `materializedata()` would leave `stablesignature` and other config-derived state stale unless the code rebuilds `timelinedata` from the updated config.

undo reconciliation: step 16's post-commit `reconcilepinnedgroups` would create extra save/history events for a single delete unless it is explicitly coalesced with the triggering edit or marked to skip history.

pinned-group track sync: cross-track drag/move paths must also update `pinnedshotgroup.trackid`, but the revised plan only discusses expanding `draggedclipids` and does not call out metadata updates for the move commits.
scope management: marking shot drop and auto-sync as deferrable follow-ups narrows the deliverable below the original task brief unless the split is explicitly approved.

are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?: one `must` criterion is still an implementation-detail proxy rather than an outcome: "new timelineeditmutation variant { type: 'pinnedshotgroups' } compiles and routes correctly in applyedit." it is verifiable, but it measures an internal mechanism rather than the user-facing persistence/undo behavior the feature actually needs.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked the revised execution order and assumptions against the approved brief and idea doc. the plan still says phase 6 shot drop and phase 7 auto-sync can be deferred, but those behaviors are part of the requested feature rather than optional polish, so this is still a real scope reduction unless the split is explicitly approved.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: checked step 18 against the actual commit pipeline in `usetimelinecommit` and `usetimelinehistory`. the plan still handles deleted pinned-group clips with a post-commit follow-up `applyedit`, which would create a second commit, second save scheduling pass, and likely a second undo boundary for one user action unless it also specifies coalescing or `skiphistory` behavior.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked step 2.4 against `materializedata()` in `usetimelinecommit` and `assembletimelinedata()` in `timeline-data.ts`. the plan still says to overwrite `pinnedshotgroups` after `materializedata()` returns, but `assembletimelinedata()` computes `stablesignature` from the config it receives, so mutating the config afterward would leave signature-derived state stale unless the data is rebuilt from the updated config.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the grouped-drag plan against `useclipdrag` and `usetimelinetrackmanagement.movecliptorow()`. the plan fixes the pointer-down selection regression, but it still does not say how `pinnedshotgroup.trackid` is updated when a grouped drag or move lands on a different row, so the overlay and menu state can still point at the old track after a successful cross-track move.

are the proposed changes technically correct?: checked the cross-track multi-drag new-track branch in `useclipdrag`. that path still commits through `applyedit({ type: 'config', resolvedconfig })` using `buildconfigfromdragresult()`, and the revised plan does not explain how pinned-group metadata is updated atomically on that path, so grouped drags that create a new track are still under-specified even beyond the stale-`trackid` problem.

search for related code that handles the same concept. is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?: checked the movement surface beyond `useclipdrag`. the broader problem is not just drag-threshold expansion; both grouped multi-drag and single-clip cross-track move helpers can relocate clips across rows, so every move path that changes a pinned group's row membership has to update pinned-group metadata too, and the revised plan still scopes that too narrowly.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: checked the move infrastructure that exists today. `useclipdrag` handles grouped moves, `usetimelinetrackmanagement.movecliptorow()` handles single-clip cross-track moves, and the plan still does not call out patching `pinnedshotgroup.trackid` in either commit path, so the new data model is not wired through every location that can change clip rows.

does the change touch all locations and supporting infrastructure?: the revised plan now has duplicate shot-drop and auto-sync sections: steps 13-14 contain todo placeholders, while steps 21-24 contain the concrete later-phase versions. that duplication is not just cosmetic; it leaves the supporting infrastructure and file ownership for those phases ambiguous enough that the plan is still not fully actionable.

find the callers of the changed function. what arguments do they actually pass? does the fix handle all of them?: checked the actual commit/history callers around step 18. `commitdata()` emits `beforecommit` and schedules save on every edit, and `usetimelinehistory.onbeforecommit()` pushes undo entries per commit boundary, so a reconcile-after-delete `applyedit` still fragments one user action into multiple history/save operations unless the plan also changes those caller contracts.

grouped drag new-track path: cross-track grouped drags that create a new track still commit through the resolved-config path, and the revised plan does not explain how pinned-group metadata is updated atomically on that branch.

plan execution: the revised plan now contains duplicate shot-drop and auto-sync phases, and the earlier copies still contain todo placeholders, so those parts of the plan are not fully actionable yet.

did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: the revised plan introduced new todo placeholders for step 13 shot drop and step 14 auto-sync while also keeping later concrete shot-drop and auto-sync phases. that means two first-class behaviors from the brief are still not fully planned in an execution-ready way, even before considering whether they are deferred.

find the callers of the changed function. what arguments do they actually pass? does the fix handle all of them?: checked the multi-drag callers in `useclipdrag`. the 'new track' grouped-drag branch still routes through `buildconfigfromdragresult()` and `applyedit({ type: 'config' })`, so the revised plan still does not fully handle every caller shape for grouped moves once pinned-group metadata becomes part of the timeline state.

timeline shot-group cleanup: the plan only updates `useshotgroups.test.ts`, but the repository also has direct test coverage for the removed `resolvefinalvideoshotids()` helper and the `timelinecanvas` suggested-group/finalvideoshotids api. without explicitly updating `timelineeditor.test.ts` and `timelinecanvas.test.tsx`, the plan leaves the supporting test surface behind and the post-change suite will fail on deleted symbols and obsolete expectations.

final-video duration handling: recurring debt: step 4 still swaps in a new final-video asset without any duration source, so a shorter regenerated final video can be asked to play for the old group span and overrun the real media length.

final-video refresh path: the plan assumes `useshotfinalvideos()` refreshes every 30s, but in this repo it does not poll; stale detection only refreshes when some other invalidation fires. the badge/update flow is under-specified unless the implementation explicitly relies on the existing invalidation path or adds refetching.
plan specifies wrong data source (usevideooutputs/shot_generations) for final video availability check
plan specifies wrong mutation (useremoveimagefromshot) for final video deletion

timeline-video-duration: the plan routes dropped videos through a new generation path but does not preserve duration metadata in the asset registry. in this repo, inserted video clips fall back to 5 seconds when `registry.duration` is missing, and the existing multi-generation video path explicitly patches duration from drag metadata before dropping. following the plan as written would still create generation-backed videos that are timed incorrectly on the timeline.

asset-panel-upload-routing: step 3 names `assetpanel.tsx`, but the actual file-upload behavior is behind the `onuploadfiles` callback and `useassetoperations.uploadfiles()`, which currently sends every file straight through `uploadasset()`. updating `assetpanel` alone will not create generation-backed registry entries for uploaded videos or attach `generationid`; the plan needs to touch the callback plumbing that sits behind the panel upload button.

video-thumbnail-helper-surface: the implementation step calls for importing `extractthumbnailfromvideo` from `videothumbnailgenerator.ts`, but that helper is private in this repository. if executed literally, that step will not compile; the public surface today is `extractanduploadthumbnailonly()` or the file-based `extractvideoposterframe()` helper.
